ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Nwagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔba sɔ si nnɛ i Ɣaa ɔɣɛ ɔkigu i ɔ̃ kanyaɖidze ɔwɛ̃ iso aaba i ne ame sɔ,
translated: When food comes to the house today, God's work that he has done for us is not for one person,

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.

translated: When Herod died, God's angel appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Ne ɔso Yosef ɔta ɔkɔ ɔbi gu ɔnyi marui makɛlɛ Israel karɔ̃ iso.
translated: And so Joseph took his two sons to his father Israel.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Mite i mi nsɛgbai ame sɔ miɔfiniki mibɔrɛ i mi akpi ame.
translated: We should not be jealous of people so that we can live with them in peace.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Awu wa i Yohanes ɔpia ne, kapuipɔngɔ siwɛrɛ̃ masu mawe ala, fiɛ ɔsu bɔi ɔko kafiami ɔtɔ so ɔsɔ. Maɣendu gu kugbadzedzei loɖe ɔ̃ aɖera.
translated: When John's disciples came, they took the body and buried it in a tomb. Then they went and told Jesus.

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Ɔ̃iwe i Nasaret, ɣɛɛ ɔkɛlɛ Kapernaum, ɔmagɛ̃ gɔ nse i Galilea itubu kanya i Zebulon gu Naftali karɔ̃ iso.
translated: He left Nazareth and went to live in Capernaum, a town by the sea in the region of Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Mmɔ i Tsirise ɔba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Si fɔ nɖe Ɣaa Ɔbi ne, ɣere ata wagbe sɔ akpese aɖera.”
translated: "When Christ comes, he will say, 'If you love God, then you must love your neighbor.'"

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: “Mi nse lɛ mma mitã maturi ɔɖuɖu, ɣɛɛ si mma ɔyu me ɔmɛrɛ̃ ne, maisibawo ɔri kuwɛ̃ iso ɔki ɔpia me ɔmɛrɛ̃. Misiina kusia kukuwɛ̃ iɖo sɔ maasu me mawiri maturi ɔsɛ me iso.
translated: I know that when my children mature physically, but they are not yet mature in mind, sometimes bad things happen to them in their minds. So I want to train them to mature in their thinking as well.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Ɔwiɔ ne minɔ masɛ maɣɛ ana sɔ, ‘Daatu ndamu, Ɣɛɛ bara i nnɛ aɣɛ sɔ aabara atã Ɣaa iso.’
translated: He/She said to me, 'My daughter, God has shown you that he/she will do all God's will.'

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: “Minɔ masɛ maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Si abiɛ fɔ ɔlaa inɔmi ne, maabiɛ fɔ ire, fiɛ si akpa fɔ ɔlaa inyɛ ne, maakpa fɔ ire fɔ wũ.’
translated: And I say to them that, 'If you do to the least of these, you have done to me, and if you don't do to the least of these, you have not done to me also.'

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Si misɛ miɖɔɛ mma nsɛ maɖɔɛ mi ɔnɔwɛ̃ ne, be kuso miafɔ i Ɣaa kɔrɛ? Ma lampofɔdze ma wũ masɛ mabara ngbɔ.
translated: If I had not done among them the works that no one else did, they would not be guilty. But now they have seen and yet they have hated both me and my Father.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Fiɛ si mito mikparama kayi ne, midaase lɛ mma nsɛ mabara ara si mabo maturi anɔ. Masɛ mabie sɔ maata maya makparama kayi i kasɔrɛkɔ̃ gu kuɣa ame sɔ maturi si manya ma. Lobie sɔ lotã mi itsue sɔ ma kuso ame maɔfɔ mmɔ ne.
translated: When we were young, we were taught that we should respect our elders. I was told that I should greet my elders in the morning before they greet me. It is good that you also do the same so that people will respect you.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Ɣɛɛ fɔ si ato akparama kayi ne, kɛlɛ fɔ iyo kame si asɛ̃ iyo apia so fiɛ aakpere fɔ Ɔse gɔ maisɛ manya ala anɔmi, iyɔ gɔ ɔ̃ ɔsɛ ɔnya ta awɛrɛra ne, ɔto ɔ̃atã-ɔ kuso.
translated: If we do not follow the commandments, but we do things that are not pleasing to God, and we do not obey his word, he will not bless us in our work, and we will suffer.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Fɔ sigara iɖe si siba i bo ndɛ̃. Maabara fɔ kuɖɔɛ i kayiiso lɛ kumɛgɔ masɛ mabara i kato.
translated: They bought cigarettes and tobacco and things like that. But they didn't go to school because there was no money to pay for it.

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: Ne loatã sɔ maturi iibatsue sɔ ato anyi kanya, ɣɛɛ fɔ Ɔse gɔ maisɛ manya, fiɛ ɔsɛ ɔnya ara wɛrɛa aatã-ɔ kuso. Kato Nɖe Ara Ka Kɔlakɔ̃ Sɛɛ.
translated: When the time came for the students to go home for vacation, they said God should help them, so that each of them would return safely. That is How People Should Live.

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Ɣɛɛ nnɛ iso i mi anɔ si arɛtɛ nɖe sɔ Ɣaa si ɔɖe sigara i mi iso gu si mibara sibɔlɔlɔ ara iyɔ ɔto ɔ̃asu ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔtã mi.
translated: If you do not have money to buy God's word or cigarettes, you should not have money to buy alcohol or other bad things.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Be ɔso mito mimararã so i nnɛ miapia iso? Minyɔ ipo ame adziri kumɛgɔ asɛ amɔ. Aisɛ ata ɖɔti ɣee awe sikati.
translated: So you people don't know what you are doing today? You should find someone who knows how to do it well. Because if not, it will spoil and waste money.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Ɣee ɔsu aɣɛ ɔtã ɔ̃ ɔbi si ɔkarɛ wũ ikpɛ?
translated: When water becomes scarce, does one bathe with palm oil?

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Kado ɔpɛ, siwore ɔyi, awɛ ɔkpɛ̃ teteree ɣɛɛ iyo iirɔ̃ ala mapia ne karɔ̃ ame kukaakɔ.
translated: So then, the Siwu person, when he wants to do something good, he must first think about it well before he does it.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”

translated: "The next day when he came he said to him, 'Ratedze, the thing you asked for yesterday is not there.'"

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,

translated: When he heard that Jesus had come from Judea to Galilee, he went to him and begged him to come down and heal his son, for he was about to die.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.

translated: When Jesus asked them, they all answered, and they said to him, "We believe that you are the Christ!"

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: “Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, maturi to maaɖi ma manyii matã sɔ maɖoe fiɛ mase ana to maaɖi ma mabi matã. Mabi to maata maya ma maɣese iso maɖi ma matã maɖoe.
translated: When someone is sick, I will not go and see that person alone so that I can tell it to others, but I will go and see that person with others. With others so that we can go together and we can all see that person together.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Rasuãdze kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔɖo ɔ̃ ratedze, fiɛ ɔɖabo kuwɛ̃ ana iisɛ ɔɖo ɔ̃ ɔkɔse.
translated: When a man marries a woman, then that woman becomes his wife.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.

text: Ne ɔso midaanigã alasɔ mifɔ kuɣa miɖo kunagini. Idaakpɛ̃-ɔ Kunuarɛ Sɔ Aɖe Yesu Ɔre.

text: Ɔbiara gɔ loabie sɔ ɔ̃akɔkɔ ɔ̃ ngbã to ɔ̃ayura me, ɣɛɛ ɔbiara gɔ loasu ɔ̃ ngbã ɔtã ala wũ ɔso to ɔ̃ana ngbã. Ɣaa To Ɔ̃atã Ɔbiara Gɔ Lofɔ Yesu Masiaidze Kuso.
translated: Everyone should know that God's kingdom is not in words, but everyone should show God's power in their life. That Is Why Everyone Should Believe In Jesus Christ.

translated: But John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say he has a demon.

ref: You people are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other,

translated: Today, if someone asks you about your character/behavior, can you say that you have done things that you are not ashamed of? If you cannot say that you are clean,

translated: When Jesus saw that the crowd was following him, he had compassion on them. He taught them many things and healed their sick.

translated: We are not lying to you! Or do you think that what we are saying is not true at all? But we are telling you the truth so that you will know it well.

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.

translated: He then went to the farm to meet the person. He greeted him saying, "Good afternoon, I have come to greet you."

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose even what little they have.
text: Alasɔ ngɔ mba ara ne, maatã wũ ara ɔ̃ ara ɔsi abua, ɣɛɛ ngɔ ina ne, kɛkɛĩ gɔ ame ɔba ne, maamɔɛ̃ mafɔ wũ iso.
translated: When the time comes, I will gather all my children together, and when that happens, even if someone comes, they won't be able to separate us again.

ref: But the girl's mother told her to say, “Here on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ɔbirɔ̃gomi ɔnyi ɔɣere wũ sɔ ɔkarɛ Ndupiedze Yohanes iti. Ne ɔso ɔsɛ ɔ̃aɣere igara sɔ, “Tã me Ndupiedze Yohanes iti i kategelei iso ngbe kiniɔ.”
translated: One day he went to visit John the Baptist. And when he saw him he said, "This is John the Baptist whom I have been looking for all this time."

ref: But they said, “We have only five small loaves of bread and two fish.”

translated: "When the woman arrived there, she said, 'The bread and the meat in the basket are for you to share and eat.'"

ref: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
text: Ɔɣere maturi sɔ masɛ i karɔ̃ i sikua iso fiɛ ka ɔsu abolo abo aru gu akpɛ fɛ̃a anyɔ, ɔnyɔ kato ɔpɛ Ɣaa siba. Ɔbɛbɛse ɔsu ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ masu maɣɛɣɛ maturi.
translated: He went and took a cutlass and cut a stick and carved it into a spoon and cooked porridge and ate it all, and when he finished eating he thanked God. He always cooked food for himself because he had no wife.

text: Ne ɔso marasuãdze ma i ɔkolo ame ɔpɛ agɛgɛ̃ matã Yesu maɣɛ sɔ, “Gbaã ne, Ɣaa Ɔbi aɖe!” Yesu Ɔsa Manyɛse I Genezaret.
translated: When the people in the boat saw all that Jesus had done, they said, "Truly, you are the Son of God!" Jesus Heals the Sick in Gennesaret.

ref: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.

translated: Jesus answered them, "Whatever I do in my Father's name, that is what testifies about me."

translated: If you do not follow my commandments, you will disobey God's law, and you will not receive my blessings.

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.

translated: But Jesus knew their thoughts. And he said to the man with the withered hand, "Get up and stand in the middle!"

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Ɣɛɛ atɔ̃me wa nsɛ abɔrɛ i ɔturi kanya ame ne, ɔ̃ ɔtu ame abɔrɛ fiɛ asɛ apukutura wũ.
translated: When a woman gives birth to a child and the child cries, she carries the child on her back and ties it with a cloth.

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Yesu ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Iilɛ sɔ maasu mabiɛtɛ aɖera matã makpɛkpɛ.”
translated: "Jesus answered them, "I tell you the truth, everyone who sins is a slave to sin."

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnɔ atɔ̃me wagbe ne, ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ɔrɔ̃go, fɔ kafɔkaɖe ɔmɔ gbaã! Nnɛ ato akarɛ si iba itã-ɔ.” Ɔluwɛ̃ ɔbi birɔ̃gomi ko losarɛ. Yesu Ɔsa Maturi Gbodzoo.
translated: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, "Friend, your sins are forgiven! Now take your mat and go home." The man immediately got up and left. Jesus Heals a Man with a Withered Hand.

ref: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
text: Gɔ matara anɔ kato ne, mainya kuwɛ̃ i mmɔ, iɖo Yesu ɔnɔwɛ̃.
translated: And when I have prepared a place for you, I will come again and receive you to myself, that where I am you may be also.

ref: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, nto loɣere mi sɔ Eliya ɔɔba koko, maturi iinya wũ maɖi ite. Mabara wũ nnɛ mabie fiɛ ngbɔ ame mato maabara Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi ɔtile ana ne.”
translated: And now, you are saying that I should go tell my master that Elijah is here, so he will kill me. I have not done anything wrong that I should be handed over to King Ahab to be killed."

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: “Nto loledza mi iɣere sɔ ira biara nɛ i mi maturi inyɔ aatɔrã misia ita i kayi gagbe iso i kayi ikparama ame ne, wũ Ɔse gɔ i kato to ɔ̃abara ne ɔtã mi.
translated: Because he has loved me, therefore I will deliver him; I will set him on high because he has known my name, he will call upon me and I will answer him, God says I will be with him in trouble and I will rescue him.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Gɔ ɔɖabo gɔmɔ na sikã ɔ̃atã kuso ɔso ne, igara ɔtã sɔ marɔdɛ̃ ɔ̃ gu ɔ̃ ɔre gu mabi lɛ masande gu ira biara nɛ ɔba si masu matã kuso.
translated: So when the man went to look for all the animals, he found that the leopard had killed his child and was eating it, so he went and reported to all the animals.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: “Ne ɔso igara ɔpia makpere ɔɖabo mabɔ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, ‘Ɔɖabo lalaa aɖe! Losu fɔ kuso gɔ ato me ɔɖuɖu lotsɛ-ɔ alasɔ atã me iti.
translated: When the man saw that the woman had not yet arrived, he said, 'The woman is late! Let me cook food so that when she comes we will eat together.'

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Ma ara ɔɖo Yesu kayiri, ne ɔpɛgu ma anɔmi matsɛ inya fiɛ marui masiai wũ ne.
translated: When I heard about Jesus, I wanted him to heal my two sick children for me.

ref: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
text: Ne Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Nto loɣere mi gbaã sɔ si mifɔ miɖe fiɛ miiwararã ne, mito miawo nnɛ lobara kudziri gɔgbe ɔbara. Fiɛ iiɖe ne kere, ɣɛɛ mito miaɣere kube gɔgbe sɔ, ‘Kɛlɛ aafuĩdza so apia i ɔpo ame,’ fiɛ iaba ngbɔ.
translated: Then Jesus said to his disciples, "I tell you the truth, if you do not forgive people their sins, your Father in heaven will not forgive your sins either. When you do not forgive, you will be like someone who says, 'I will not let go of what this person did to me,' and it will be a burden."

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Alasɔ Ndupiedze Yohanes ɔba ɔ̃ate mi sibɔlɔlɔ ɔri, ɣɛɛ miifɔ wũ miɖe, ɣɛɛ ma lampofɔdze gu asɔrɔ̃maɖedze ɔfɔ wũ maɖe. Atoa sɔ minya nwagbe ne, miifinikira mi adzuni mifɔ wũ miɖe.” Yesu Ɔpɛ Igbã I Marabaradze Lalaa Mawɛ̃ Iso.
translated: When John the Baptist came to you in the way of righteousness, you did not believe him, but the tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. And even after you saw this, you did not repent and believe him." Jesus Told the Parable of the Wicked Tenants.

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Matã mmuai sɔ, “Ɔto ɔ̃aɖoe marabaradze lalaa magbe ɔsu kakpɛkɔ̃ ɔtã marabaradze mama ma loasu ɔ̃ kaɖekɔ̃ mabɔ wũ i ɔwi iso si masɔrɛ̃ ɔraɖui.”
translated: Matthew said, "The one who sowed the good seed is the Son of Man, and the field is the world, and the good seeds are the children of the kingdom, but the weeds are the children of the evil one."

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: Ɔpia ɔ̃ maɖabo sɔ masɛ maakpere mma matã itsue sɔ maba iyi kaɖekɔ̃ sɔ ka maba, ɣɛɛ masɛ̃ sɔ maito maba.
translated: One day I went to the farm to weed, and when I finished weeding and wanted to come back, I saw that it was going to rain, so I came back.

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mmɔ miɣɛrɛ ne, alasɔ miiɣe Ɔko Sekelea ame atɔ̃me fiɛ miiɣe Ɣaa ɔle ana.
translated: Jesus answered them, "Truly I tell you, you seek me not because you saw signs but because you ate the loaves and were filled."

ref: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
text: Ne Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Si igara David ɔbi ɔɖe ne, lɛ ise ɔso i David ɔki i Ɣaa Siwarã ɔle ame ɔkpere wũ sɔ Bosate? David ɔɣɛ sɔ,
translated: And Jesus answered them, saying, "Have you not read what David did, when he and those who were with him were hungry? How David entered the House of God and ate the bread of the Presence? David said,

ref: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
text: Si igara David ɔkpere wũ Bosate ne, nda i Kristo aaɖe ɔ̃ ɔbi ɔɣɛ?”
translated: If David calls him Lord, how then is he his son?

ref: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
text: Si makpere ma i ɔpɔrɔ̃ katukɔ̃ ne, masɛ maɖɔɛ kusɛkɔ̃ kɔlɛa. Masɛ maɖɔɛ masɛ i kusɛkɔ̃ sɛɛ gu kusɛkɔ̃ kɔlɛa i ma kusarɛkɔ̃ ana.
translated: If I don't have money in my pocket, I can't buy anything. I can't buy what I want because I don't have money or coins.

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: “Madaakpere mi sɔ ratedze, alasɔ mi ɔɖuɖu ne, manyii miɖe fiɛ ratedze ɔwɛ̃ saã miba.
translated: I don't have money to buy clothes, nor do I have food to eat, that's why I came to beg you people to help me.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: “Ndzo miɖe mitã mi mmara matedze gu ma Farisise! Mi ma nsɛ mibara ara sɔ si mibo maturi anɔ! Mise lɛ maturi akpibiɔ wa matsue makua kukaakɔ, ɣɛɛ wã ame ɔyi ku makpise buria ku akui.
translated: Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs, which look beautiful on the outside but on the inside are full of the bones of the dead and everything unclean.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Minyɔ ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu kukaakɔ. Alasɔ gbaã nto loɣere mi sɔ mato maabiɛbiɛ wã ɔɖuɖu gɔ ita kuiwɛ̃ iibakuti isia i ne ɔlaa iso sɔ maibasisi mapɛyu.” Yesu Ɔɣere Ɔ̃ Marasuãdze Ɔɣiã Gɔ Maaɖe.
translated: And he said to them, "I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer. For I tell you, I will not eat it again until it finds fulfillment in the kingdom of God." Jesus Instituted the Lord's Supper.

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Nwagbe ɔɖuɖu se lɛ kuɣe iɖoɖo ibo ɖeakatɔ̃.
translated: The food preparation took about three hours.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: Ne ɔso si ɔrere ɔba ɔ̃aɣere mi sɔ, ‘Nyɔ, ɔɔnya Kristo i ngbe,’ ɣee ɔrere ɔɣere mi sɔ, ‘Ɔpia i mmɔ’ ne, midaafɔ miɖe.
translated: If someone comes to you saying, 'Look, here is Christ,' or says to you, 'He is there,' do not believe it.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, marɛrɛ̃ inyɔ to maaya i kakpɛkɔ̃, Ɣaa aasu ɔwɛ̃ ɔruigu ɔnyua ɔwɛ̃ ɔɣedza.
translated: When someone dies, before they bury them in the grave, God takes the person's soul or the person's spirit.

ref: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
text: Madzimifɔ ɔsu ma sikaniɛ, ɣɛɛ maisu nnɔĩ mabua i sikaniɛ iso.
translated: The chief is not crying for money, but he is crying because he doesn't have money to spend.

ref: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
text: Iyɔ be ɔso fiɛ aisu wũ sikã asɛ aasɛ i sikã kakɔlakɔ̃ sɔ si loba ne, loasɛ loafɔ sɛ̃ ku kusia?’
translated: "If someone's child dies and they bury the child and later the child resurrects and comes back, will they not be very happy?"

ref: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
text: “Ne makɔse ɔfɔ kanya sɔ, ‘Ɔɖabo lalaa gu dãdze aɖe. Aɣe sɔ nsɛ losu aɖera i ngbegɔ loikpɛ̃ fiɛ nsɛ losa ɔraɖui kanya i ngbegɔ loisa ɔraɖui?
translated: And the priest answered the young man, saying, "The journey on which you go is before the Lord. Is it not that the way you are going and the place you are going to, the Lord knows the place you are going to?"

ref: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
text: Ne masɛ iti ame sɔ maamɔɛ̃ Yesu i iwɛrɛ ame maaɖoe.
translated: And they took him and brought him to Jesus and they put him on it.

ref: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
text: Ne gɔ maka kuka male Ɣaa ne, mabɔrɛ makɛlɛ Nnɔĩ Adziri Kube iso. Yesu Ɔɣɛ Ɔsɛ Sɔ Petro To Ɔ̃asɛ̃ Wũ.
translated: And when I went to Jerusalem, I also met with the Lord's apostles. Jesus Christ Spoke to Peter About His Work.

ref: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
text: I iyi ɖeakatɔ̃ nɛ iso masɛ maɖe Boɖoboɖo Gɔ Loita Iyi ne, marasuãdze ɔba maakarɛ Yesu sɔ, “Lɛ ato abie sɔ bowa Kukpi Ɔtakuwara Bo iyi aɖera botã-ɔ?”
translated: On the fifteenth day of the month of Boɖoboɖo, they asked Jesus, saying, "Why do your disciples not keep the tradition of the elders?"

ref: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
text: Mita si borui! Minyɔ, miɔnya ngɔ nto ɔɖi me ɔtã.” Ma Yudase Ɔmɔɛ̃ Yesu.
translated: Let us go also! We will die with him there." But Judas Betrayed Jesus.

ref: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
text: Mma lomɔɛ̃ Yesu ɔkɔ wũ makɛlɛgu Kayafa iyo ngbegɔ i mmara matedze gu kaɖe makpakpa ɔsakanya.
translated: Then they took Jesus to the house of Caiaphas, the high priest, where the teachers of the law and the elders had assembled.

ref: and said, “You think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!” Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Mark
text: to maɣere wũ sɔ, “Gɔ fɔ nɖe Kristo ne, ɣere bo ngɔ lopɛ-ɔ!” Petro Ɔsɛ̃ Sɔ Ɔ̃iɣe Yesu.
translated: They mocked him saying, "If you are the Christ, tell us who hit you!" Peter Denies That He Knows Jesus.

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Iidzoro fiɛ marɛrɛ̃ ma nɣɛ i mmɔ ɔɣere Petro sɔ, “Fɔ wũ ma ndɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ aɖe ala fɔ ikaɖe ɔɖi-ɔ itã!”
translated: When the servant girl saw Peter again, she said to those standing there, "This man is one of them!"

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
text: Ne Petro ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Lokã sɔ ɔnukuare nto loɣɛ! Ɣaa si ɔkpadzɛ̃ me kutsue si iiɖe gbaã iɖe sɔ loiɣe ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔmɔ!” Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, kɔkɔ ɔɖi kanya.
translated: Then Peter said, "Lord, if it is you, command me to come to you on the water!" He said to him, "Come." And Peter got down from the boat.

ref: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
text: Ɔfɔ Ɣaa ɔɖe fiɛ ɔto ɔɣɛ sɔ ɔ̃ nɖe Ɣaa Ɔbi mɔmɔ, mitã bonyɔ si Ɣaa aaso ɔba ɔ̃aɖi wũ!”
translated: He said God should come and see how he is suffering so that God should have mercy on him, because of the way God loves children he should help him!

ref: Then they sat down to guard him.
text: Ne kama ne, masɛ i mmɔ mato madzuɛ̃ wũ.
translated: If it happens like that, I will not be able to do anything.

ref: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
text: Gɔ kpakpɛ̃dze ɔkpakpa gu makpakpɛ̃dze ma nɣɛ mato madzuɛ̃ Yesu ɔnya karɔ̃ iti gu ira biara nɛ loba ne, maɣɛ i ifɔ̃ ame sɔ, “Gbaã ne, Ɣaa Ɔbi ɔɖe i ngbe!”
translated: And when the unclean spirit had torn him and cried with a loud voice, he came out of him, and they were all amazed, insomuch that they questioned among themselves, saying, "What thing is this? A new doctrine!"

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Yesu gu ɔ̃ masiaidze magbe ɔkɛlɛ ɔmagɛ̃ gɔ marɔ Kapernaum. Ma Yudase Iwarãyi ne, Yesu ɔsɛ ma kasarɛkɔ̃ ɔsɛ ɔ̃ate Ɣaa Itɔ̃me.
translated: Jesus and his disciples went and stayed in Capernaum. On the Jewish Sabbath, Jesus went to the synagogue to worship God.

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Ibara mma lokã wũ atsue ɣii, alasɔ ɔte ara lɛ ngɔ mba ɔle, iiɖe lɛ ma Yudase mmara matedze awe.
translated: When they had finished eating, the devil had already put it into the heart of Judas Iscariot to betray him.

ref: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
text: Gɔ Yesu to ɔbɔrɛ i ndu ame ne, ɔnya kato ɔbusi, Siwarã Bielea ɔso sisia wũ iso lɛ ilɔpɔ.
translated: When Jesus finished speaking to the people, Simon Peter, the leader of the disciples, came forward.

ref: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ɔsarɛ, ɔ̃ iso ɔse sekelee.
translated: When a person is sick, they don't go to school.

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Maturi gbodzoo ɔba mmɔ gɔ kukpa kanɛkɔ̃ iisiwe i iyo nɛ kame mapia gu kayogodɔ̃. Ne ɔsɛ i mmɔ ɔɣɛ Itɔ̃me Bielea ne.
translated: The Maturi chief came and said that the elders should gather together so that they could discuss an important matter. And he said that matter was about Itɔ̃me Bielea.

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: “Kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔta ɔkati kuere ala kakatisɛi ɣɛtɛ. Si abara ngbɔ ne, kakatisɛi ɣɛtɛ gamɔ to kaakarĩ karui iyoi nɛ loabɛ iɖo iɖeakatɔ̃.
translated: In the past, when someone died, they would perform funeral rites. But nowadays, funeral rites have become very expensive and difficult for people to organize.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔ kɔrɔ̃ lokarĩ sɔ ɔta ɔya i ma katɔ̃.
translated: Jesus went and stood by the lake so that people would come to him.

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Ne mma lomini wũ i mmɔ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Nyɔ, fɔ ɔnyi gu manyiibi ɣɛ i inyamɔ mato makpere-ɔ!”
translated: "And the woman said to her husband, "Look, the man of God who always passes by us!"

ref: So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
text: Ne ɔso si Ɔbosam sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ɔta siɣɛ so iso fiɛ siɣɛɣɛ so ne, ɔ̃ sigarakaɖekɔ̃ gamɔ iibawo ɔya, siɔba kawirikɔ̃.
translated: When the Devil saw that he could not defeat him in any way, he brought out a cigarette and gave it to him, saying he should smoke it.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, imusuora biara nɛ i ɔturi aaɣɛ i Siwarã Bielea iso ne, Ɣaa iibasu ɔtsɛ wũ kuiyikuiyi. Maibasu, aɣɛrɛ wagbe matsɛ manya.”
translated: And when the Muslims came to attack the Swahili Coast, God protected them completely. They did not succeed, because we prayed and God heard us.

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: Matsɛrɛ masɛ i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ, ‘Mato maanya, ɣɛɛ maibanya maɖi ite. Mato maanɔ, ɣɛɛ maibanɔ karɔ̃, sɔ si maibafiniki fiɛ Ɣaa aasu ma akpi ɔtsɛ ma.’ ”
translated: The elders said to Oko Sekelea, "We have heard, but we do not understand. We have heard, but we do not know the reason, so we want you to explain God's word to us."

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: Si abi ɔrɛtɛ ne, kpɛ̃dze to ɔ̃asɔrɛ̃, alasɔ ɔraɖui ɔsɔrɛ̃wi ɔɔɣo.” Igbã Isegu Idziribi Kosoi Iso.
translated: If you do that, then you will be blessed, because you will do God's will." This is the Word of the Lord our God.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ marɔ Yairo fiɛ ɔɖe ma Yudase kasarɛkɔ̃ ɔkpakpa ɔba mmɔ. Gɔ ɔnya Yesu ne, ɔpɛ agɛgɛ̃ i ɔ̃ katɔ̃.
translated: When he arrived at Jairus's house, he did not allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the child's father and mother.

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: Ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Nyɔ kumɛgɔ i maturi ɔki malɔ bo. Be ɔso fiɛ ato akarɛ sɔ nna lopɛgu-ɔ?”
translated: "He asked his friend, saying, "Why are you standing there doing nothing? Or is there no work to do today?"

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ba kuwarɛ i kakɔi biara iɖo ɔ̃ mɔmɔ ɔmagɛ̃, ɔ̃ maturi gu ɔ̃ iyo maturi anɔmi ame.”
translated: Jesus answered them, "Truly I tell you, everyone who commits sin is a slave to sin."

ref: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
text: Ɔsu ne ɔsia i kategelei iso, ɔsu ɔba ɔ̃atã ɔbitɛ gɔgbe. Ne ɔfɔ, ɔsu ɔkɛlɛgu ɔ̃ ɔnyi ne.
translated: When a person is sick in bed, the person becomes very weak. And also, the person cannot do anything.

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mi mɔmɔ mitã ma irere maɖe.” Ne maɣere wũ sɔ, “Nnɛmɔ to iafɔ lɛ ɔturi awa farafana kuso. Ato abie sɔ bonina ngbɔ itã ma aɖera?”
translated: Jesus answered them, "You also ask me one question." Then he said to them, "Where did John's baptism come from, from heaven or from people? What do you think?"

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Manigã gbaã sɔ manya wũ i ndu iso. Ne Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Midaanigã! Mme loɖe. Mina ɔtu.”
translated: The woman had been sick for twelve years. And Jesus said to her, "Daughter! Be well. Go in peace."

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ Yesu ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔya mabo i ɔkolo ame sɔ mawara makɛlɛ Betsaida. Ne ɔ̃ wũ ka ɔɖi iturikuri ɔri ne.
translated: When Jesus and his disciples came out of the boat at a place called Bethsaida, they brought a blind man to him.
